data.title: Gold Futures Inch Lower But Hold Higher on Week — Market Talk
data.description:
0751 GMT - Gold futures tick lower, though they hold to higher ground after U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell struck a dovish tone on the prospect of monetary policy easing. Futures are down 0.2% at $3,412.90 a troy ounce, but remain up more than 1% on week. Powell's speech at the Jackson Hole symposium before the weekend reassured markets that the Fed is tilted toward cutting interest rates rather than renewed tightening, supporting the appeal of non-interest-bearing bullion, Pepperstone's Ahmad Assiri says in a note. Near-term, the release of Personal Consumption Expenditure data could weigh on the U.S. dollar and boost gold further, Assiri says. Even more crucially, further signs of softness in next week's Nonfarm Payroll report would strengthen the case for cuts and amplify gold demand, Assiri adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0727 GMT - The Monetary Authority of Singapore could loosen its monetary policy as July's CPI print continues the trend of cooling inflation, says Josh Gilbert, a market analyst at eToro, in an email. Core inflation rose 0.5% in July, compared to June's 0.6% increase. It appears to be the right move for MAS to ease further in October, with core inflation cooling and growth likely moderating in 2H. The central bank could also wait for more evidence after holding steady at its July meeting. "The conversation is shifting from bringing inflation under control to supporting growth," Gilbert says.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0713 GMT - Eurozone government bond yields move higher in early trade as investors await German Ifo business sentiment index at 0800 GMT. Ifo's business-climate index is forecast at 88.3 in August versus 88.6 in July, according to the Wall Street Journal's poll of analysts. Commerzbank Research says the data are likely to show a "continuous sluggish uptrend" which is unlikely to revive expectations of interest-rate cuts by the European Central Bank. A bond auction in Belgium could also attract attention for eurozone bond investors, although trading volumes will be lower than usual due to Monday's U.K. bank holiday. The 10-year German Bund yield rises 2.6 basis points to 2.746%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0701 GMT - The U.S. dollar edges higher against a basket of currencies in European morning trade, recovering modestly after sharp falls on Friday as investors adjust to expectations of Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts. Fed Chair Jerome Powell's speech at the Jackson Hole Symposium opened the door to a rate cut next month, causing a sharp jump in rate-cut bets and a slide in the dollar. U.S. money markets now price in an 84% chance of a September rate cut, up from around 70% before Powell's speech. Investors will continue to pay attention to upcoming Fed commentary, which could show divisions among Fed policymakers "remain evident," Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. The DXY dollar index edges up 0.1% to 97.762, having dropped to a near four-week low of 97.556. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0617 GMT - The U.S. Treasury market's reaction to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech "felt like an overdone reaction," says Nomura Capital Management CIO Matthew Pallai. "There is still a fairly high degree of uncertainty, and as Powell mentioned, the path for rates is not necessarily on a preset path," he says. Risk markets are priced for a meaningful easing cycle, and that doesn't tie out with the level of uncertainty, Pallai says. He found Powell's tone as "generally balanced," though he clearly kept a rate cut in September on the table. Nomura Capital Management continues to expect one or two Fed rate cuts this year, subject to data. Two-year Treasury yields fell 10.4 basis points to 3.688% on Friday, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0554 GMT - The five- to 30-year segment of the U.S. Treasury curve has room to steepen, says Citi. "We prefer five- to 30-year steepeners due to the less punitive carry compared to two- to 10-year," says strategist Jason Williams in a note. This is combined with Citi's view that two-year yields may have limited room to rally in the short term unless nonfarm payrolls data are weak, he says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0545 GMT - The Federal Reserve appears to be on track to lower its policy rate in the near term, albeit with a cautious approach, says Pimco's Richard Clarida in a note. Fed Chair Jerome Powell's much-anticipated speech at the annual Jackson Hole Symposium last week did not disappoint markets, nor did the Fed's rollout of its revised monetary policy framework surprise Fed watchers, the global economic advisor says. "Over the longer term, the Fed reaffirmed its commitment to its dual mandate while introducing only nuanced clarifications to its monetary policy framework," says Clarida, who was the Fed's Vice Chair from 2018 to 2022. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0544 GMT - Citi Research slightly updates some of its forecasts for the year-end yield levels of U.S. Treasurys, while it is confident with its long-held forecast for the 10-year yield, says strategist Jason Williams in a note. It continues to expect the year-end level of the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.10%, sticking to its forecast it has held since last year, he says. However, Citi modestly updates the other benchmark points to better fit its view for a steeper curve and lower policy rate expectation to be priced for 2026, Williams says. Citi's new base case for the two-year Treasury yield is 3.50%; it is 3.65% for the five-year yield and 4.70% for the 30-year yield. The 10-year Treasury yield rises 1.3 bps to 4.270% in Asian trade, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0413 GMT - Bitcoin's near-term outlook will largely depend on upcoming U.S. economic data, including preliminary GDP and core PCE inflation, XS.com market analyst Linh Tran writes in a commentary. The Federal Reserve would have stronger grounds to begin a rate-cutting cycle if the data continues to indicate a slowing U.S. economy and easing inflation. "This scenario would create a liquidity-rich environment favorable for bitcoin's recovery," Tran says. However, if the data comes in unexpectedly "hot," investors could maintain a defensive stance,prolonging the short-term correction, Tran adds. Bitcoin is last up 0.2% at $112,981, LSEG data shows.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0328 GMT - The Philippine central bank's tone is likely to stay relatively dovish on Thursday as a "more accommodative" monetary policy stance remains warranted, Nomura economists write in a report. The central bank is also expected to cut its policy rate by 25 bps. There remains a still-benign inflation outlook after a lower-than-expected CPI reading in July. The Philippine economy's output gap is negative despite a slight improvement in 2Q GDP growth. The BSP is also expected to maintain its inflation forecasts and its assessment that inflation expectations remain well anchored, Nomura adds. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0308 GMT - The Singapore dollar weakens slightly against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session on a likely technical correction following broad-based strength across regional currencies on Fed Chair Powell's dovish remarks last Friday. However, the Singapore dollar's weakness will probably be limited by ongoing Fed rate-cut hopes, analysts say. Powell signaled that he may be open to lowering rates, four members of CBA Global Economic & Markets Research say. Hence, CBA now expects the Fed will next cut rates in September versus its earlier forecast of a rate reduction in October. USD/SGD is 0.1% higher at 1.2831. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0303 GMT - The Bank of Korea is likely to stand pat on interest rates for a second consecutive time at Thursday's policy meeting. In a Wall Street Journal poll of 27 economists, 20 say they don't expect a rate change, while the remaining seven forecast a rate cut. A dovish hold would give the BOK space to monitor household debt trends, evaluate the effects of continuing fiscal stimulus, and track the Fed's policy stance, Goldman Sachs economists led by Goohoon Kwon say in a note. GS expects the BOK to cut rates in October. Most economists expect the BOK to slightly raise its 2025 GDP and inflation forecasts, citing stronger-than-expected 2Q growth data. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

data.title: FGV Holdings says current operations will continue as planned after delisting
data.description:
FGV Holdings, one of the world's biggest palm oil producers, said on Monday that its delisting from the Malaysian bourse will allow it to operate with greater agility and align more closely with its major shareholder.

Its current operations, stakeholders' commitments, and community-focused initiatives will continue as planned with no disruption to business activities, the company said in a statement.

FGV will officially delist from the main board on August 28 after 12 years as a publicly listed company. The company was taken over by a state-owned body, the Federal Land Development Authority (Felda), after it had gained more than the 90% of the company's shares required for a takeover.

"This is a strategic move that strengthens our alignment with Felda. It positions us to focus on sustainable growth, operational excellence, and long-term value creation for our stakeholders," said group chief executive officer Fakhrunniam Othman.

"We remain fully committed to moving forward together with Felda for the benefit of our employees, settlers, and the communities we serve," he added.

data.title: Fed Rate Cut in September Will Still Depend on Upcoming Data — Market Talk
data.description: An interest-rate cut by the Federal Reserve in September is likely but not certain after the central bank's chair Jerome Powell cleared a path to a reduction in a speech on Friday, Barclays economists say in a note. "The door for another hold is not closed," and will depend on upcoming jobs and inflation data for August, they say. Unexpectedly firm jobs data combined with higher-than-expected inflation could result in rates being left on hold. Still, there is a "high bar" for this scenario and high inflation alone wouldn't suffice, they say. U.S. money markets price in an 84% chance of a September rate cut, LSEG data show. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

data.title: FTSE MIB Dips at Week Start
data.description:
The FTSE MIB fell 0.3% to around 43,180 on Monday, trimming gains from the previous session to slightly retreat from an eighteen-year high, mirroring losses across most of its European peers.

Investors digested remarks from ECB President Christine Lagarde, who noted that Europe’s labor market has exceeded expectations, remaining resilient despite high inflation and several sharp interest rate hikes in recent years.

Meanwhile, US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell signaled the possibility of near-term interest rate cuts last week, boosting risk sentiment.

In Italy, investors face a busy week, with key data releases including consumer and business confidence, the final Q2 GDP reading, and preliminary August consumer inflation figures.

Among top Italian stocks, laggards included ERG (-1.3%), Telecom Italia (-0.9%), Tenaris (-0.7%), partially offset by strong gains in the financial sector, including Mediobanca (+1.4%) and Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena (+1.9%).

data.title: European Stocks Trade Lower
data.description:
European stocks traded lower on Monday, as investors digested Powell’s signal that the Fed may soon cut rates, alongside mixed signals on growth.

The Stoxx 600 slipped 0.2%, with the DAX and CAC 40 down 0.4% and 0.3% respectively, while UK markets were shut for a holiday.

Orsted shares plunged 18% after US authorities ordered a halt to a major offshore wind project, jeopardizing its capital-raising plans.

By contrast, JDE Peet’s surged up to 17% after Keurig Dr Pepper agreed to acquire the Dutch coffee maker for €15.7 billion.

Investors await key inflation releases from Germany, France, Italy, and Spain later in the week, and watch upcoming earnings from European firms such as Pernod Ricard and US heavyweight Nvidia.

Markets remain cautious after last week’s renewed clarity on the US-EU trade deal, particularly for pharmaceuticals, but sentiment is dominated by uncertainty over growth and monetary policy paths.

data.title: NIO Shares Rise Sharply After Strong Orders for New Model
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

NIO's shares rose sharply amid optimistic bets that the Chinese electric-vehicle maker's new models will help boost sales.

Its Hong Kong-listed shares rose 17% to 53.65 Hong Kong dollars, equivalent to US$6.87, on Monday afternoon, on track for their largest one-day gain since May 2024.

The shares rose after the company recorded strong sales following the release of a new version of its ES8 model last week.

"I think it's still driven by the new ES8. Foot traffic in showrooms were quite decent over the weekend," Morningstar analyst Vincent Sun said.

NIO released a new version of its premium flagship ES8 sport-utility vehicle last week with a starting price of 416,800 yuan, equivalent to around US$58,166.

The company also had a successful launch of its Onvo L90 SUV in early August with a cheaper price tag of 265,800 yuan.

Positive sentiment toward the two new NIO models have boosted hopes that the carmaker's volumes would rise.

Investors had been concerned about whether NIO can successfully scale up due to relatively weaker sales, compared with emerging EV peers XPeng and Li Auto.

The company reported a wider first-quarter net loss of 6.89 billion yuan, compared with the 5.36 billion yuan loss a year earlier, weighed by higher operating expenses.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Turkey Factory Capacity Use Falls to Over Two-Year Low
data.description:
The manufacturing industry in Turkey operated at 73.5% of its production capacity in August 2025, marking a third consecutive monthly decline from 74.2% in the previous month.

This was the lowest reading since March 2023, as capacity utilization contracted in investment goods (68.1% vs 71.1% in July), durable consumer goods (70.3% vs 71.4%), food and beverages (73.0% vs 73.3%), and consumer goods (72.1% vs 72.3%).

Meanwhile, capacity utilization was unchanged in non-durable consumer goods (72.5%) and intermediate goods (74.7%).

data.title: Buyback of Class B shares in Essity during week 34, 2025
data.description:
Essity

Between August 18, 2025, and August 22, 2025, Essity Aktiebolag (publ) repurchased a total of 390,489 own Class B shares (ISIN: SE0009922164) under the framework of the buyback program as resolved by the Board of Directors.

The share purchase is part of the SEK 3bn buyback program announced by Essity on April 23, 2025. The buyback program will extend from April 24, 2025, until the 2026 Annual General Meeting and be implemented in accordance with the EU Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) and the European Commission’s Delegated Regulation 2016/1052 (Safe Harbour Regulation).

The share repurchase is financed using cash flow from current operations after the ordinary dividend with the ambition to continue with share buybacks over time as a recurring part of Essity’s capital allocation.

Class B shares in Essity were repurchased as follows:

DateAggregated daily volume (no. of shares):Weighted average price per day (SEK):Total daily transaction value (SEK):
August 18, 202571,907249.547317,944,198
August 19, 202572,534251.480818,240,908
August 20, 202580,584254.440620,503,841
August 21, 202582,810254.009921,034,560
August 22, 202582,654255.377221,107,947
Total accumulated during week 34 2025390,489253.096698,831,454
Total accumulated during the buyback program5,349,448260.90141,395,678,258

All purchases were conducted on Nasdaq Stockholm by BofA Securities Europe SA on behalf of Essity. Following the above purchases, Essity’s holding of treasury shares amounted on August 22, 2025, to 6,131,948 Class B shares. The total number of shares in Essity amounted on the date of this press release to 693,054,489, of which 58,973,654 Class A shares and 634,080,835 Class B shares.    

For further information, please contact:

Sandra Åberg, Vice President Investor Relations, +46 70 564 96 89, sandra.aberg@essity.com

Essity is a global, leading hygiene and health company. Every day, our products, solutions and services are used by a billion people around the world. Our purpose is to break barriers to well-being for the benefit of consumers, patients, caregivers, customers and society. Sales are conducted in approximately 150 countries under the leading global brands TENA and Tork, and other strong brands such as Actimove, Cutimed, JOBST, Knix, Leukoplast, Libero, Libresse, Lotus, Modibodi, Nosotras, Saba, Tempo, TOM Organic and Zewa. In 2024, Essity had net sales of approximately SEK 146bn (EUR 13bn) and employed 36,000 people. The company's headquarters is located in Stockholm, Sweden and Essity is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. More information at essity.com.

data.title: Renosen Pharma Acquires 1,000 Shares of Senores Pharma
Gandhar Oil Secures ₹24.60 Crore Contract from BHEL, Jhansi
Nirlon AGM to Declare ₹11/Share Dividend on September 25, 2025
data.description:
Nirlon Limited has announced that its 66th Annual General Meeting (AGM) will be held on Thursday, September 25, 2025, at 12:00 noon (IST) through video conferencing (VC) or other audio-visual means (OAVM).

The meeting will address several key items, including the adoption of audited financial statements, declaration of a final dividend, and the re-appointment of directors.

Key Agenda Items:

  • Adoption of Financial Statements: To receive, consider, and adopt the audited financial statements of the company for the financial year ended March 31, 2025, along with the reports of the directors and auditors.

  • Declaration of Final Dividend: To declare a final dividend of ₹11.00 per equity share of face value ₹10.00 each (at 110%) for the financial year ended March 31, 2025. The dividend will be distributed to shareholders whose names appear in the Register of Members of the Company as of the record date, Thursday, September 11, 2025.

  • Appointment of Director: To appoint a director in place of Mr. Arjun Khullar (DIN: 10671903), who retires by rotation at the AGM and is eligible for re-appointment.

  • Continuation of Independent Director: Approval for the continuation of Mr. Rajinder Pal Singh (DIN: 02943155) as a Non-Executive Independent Director, notwithstanding that he will exceed 75 years of age during his term.

  • Re-appointment of Executive Director & CEO: Re-appointment of Mr. Rahul V. Sagar (DIN: 00388980) as Executive Director & Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of the Company and Payment of his Managerial Remuneration for a period of five (5) years w.e.f. February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2031.

  • Appointment of Secretarial Auditors: Appointment of Alwyn Jay & Co, Practising Company Secretaries as the Secretarial Auditors for a period of five (5) consecutive years, from April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2030 and fix their remuneration.

  • Ratification of Remuneration payable to the Cost Auditor: Ratification of Remuneration payable to the Cost Auditor for the F.Y. 2025-26.

    • Details of Managerial Remuneration for Rahul V. Sagar:

    The proposed managerial remuneration for Mr. Rahul V. Sagar is detailed below:

    2030-31**

    Basic Salary per Month5,33,3335,60,0005,88,0006,17,4006,48,270

    10,21,025Total Earning per Annum16,00,000*1,00,80,0001,05,84,0001,11,13,2001,16,68,860

    70,00,000Variable Remuneration per Annum21,33,333*1,34,39,9921,41,11,9911,48,17,5911,55,58,470

    The remuneration may be subject to approval from the Central Government if required, and Mr. Sagar will receive minimum remuneration as per the Act's provisions until such approval is obtained.

    The notice includes details on e-voting, attendance through VC/OAVM, dividend distribution, and communication guidelines for members.

    The proceedings of the 66th AGM shall be deemed to be conducted at the Registered Office of the Company.

data.title: Emami share price falls 5.23% on Nifty Midcap 150
data.description:
Shares of Emami declined by 5.23% to Rs 579.95 in Monday's session, with the stock trading on the Nifty Midcap 150.

On the financial front, the consolidated revenue for the quarter-ending June 2025 stood at Rs 904.09 Crore, compared to Rs 906.07 Crore in June 2024. The net profit for the quarter-ending June 2025 was Rs 166.37 Crore, up from Rs 152.70 Crore in June 2024. The earnings per share (EPS) increased to Rs 3.76 for June 2025, from Rs 3.50 in June 2024.

The consolidated revenue for the year-ending March 2025 reached Rs 3,809.19 Crore, a notable increase compared to Rs 3,578.09 Crore in the previous year. The net profit for the year-ending March 2025 was Rs 814.55 Crore, up from Rs 727.86 Crore in March 2024. The earnings per share (EPS) also saw an increase, reaching Rs 18.48 in March 2025, compared to Rs 16.55 in March 2024.

Key financial figures from the consolidated income statement include:

AnnualMar 2025Mar 2024Mar 2023Mar 2022

Mar 2021SalesRs 3,809 CroreRs 3,578 CroreRs 3,405 CroreRs 3,192 Crore

Rs 2,880 CroreOther IncomeRs 68 CroreRs 46 CroreRs 68 CroreRs 95 Crore

The sales for the year-ending March 2025 was Rs 3,809 Crore compared to Rs 3,578 Crore in March 2024. Total expenditure stood at Rs 2,962 Crore in March 2025 compared to Rs 2,820 Crore in March 2024. Net profit increased from Rs 727 Crore in March 2024 to Rs 814 Crore in March 2025.

The company’s board announced several dividends, including an interim dividend and a special dividend of Rs 2.00 per share (200%) with an effective date of May 22, 2025. Earlier dividends include an interim dividend of Rs 4.00 per share (400%) announced on December 27, 2024, with an effective date of February 4, 2025, and another interim dividend of Rs 4.00 per share (400%) announced on October 16, 2024, effective from November 18, 2024.

Emami had previously announced bonus issues, including a 1:1 bonus on May 3, 2018, with an ex-bonus date of June 21, 2018, and a 1:2 bonus on May 6, 2013, with an ex-bonus date of June 26, 2013. Additionally, the company executed stock splits, including a split from a face value of Rs 2 to Rs 1 on July 21, 2010, and a split from Rs 10 to Rs 2 on February 16, 2004.

Moneycontrol analysis on August 18, 2025, indicated a very bullish sentiment on the stock.Shares of Emami declined by 5.23% to Rs 579.95 in Monday's session, with the stock trading on the Nifty Midcap 150.

data.title: Major Gulf markets rise on US rate cut hopes
data.description:
Major stock markets in the Gulf inched higher in early trade on Monday helped by growing expectations of U.S. Federal Reserve interest rate cuts after Chair Jerome Powell's dovish remarks last week.

Powell on Friday signalled a possible rate cut at the Fed's meeting next month, saying that risks to the job market were rising but inflation remained a threat, and that a decision wasn't set in stone.

Markets are now pricing in an 87% chance of a quarter-point rate cut in the September policy meeting, and a cumulative reduction of 48 basis points by this year-end, according to CME FedWatch Tool.

The Fed's stance holds significant implications for Gulf economies, where most currencies are pegged to the U.S. dollar, making it an anchor for regional monetary stability.

Saudi Arabia's benchmark index added 0.1%, supported by a 0.1% gain in Al Rajhi Bank and a 1.3% increase in Banque Saudi Fransi .

Dubai's main share index edged 0.1% higher, with blue-chip developer Emaar Properties rising 1.4%.

In Abu Dhabi, the index was up 0.2%.

Oil prices - a catalyst for the Gulf's financial markets - rose after Ukraine stepped up attacks on Russia, fanning concerns Russian oil supply could be disrupted, while expectations for a cut in U.S. interest rates buoyed the outlook for global growth and fuel demand.

The Qatari index gained 0.2%, with the Gulf's biggest lender Qatar National Bank climbing 0.9%.

data.title: EVE Energy's EV, ESS Battery Shipment Growth Likely to Remain Solid — Market Talk
data.description: EVE Energy's solid growth in electric vehicle and energy-storage-system battery shipments in 2Q is likely to continue in 2H, Nomura analysts say in a research report. The Chinese company's 2025 shipments of EV and ESS batteries are expected to grow by 65% to 50GWh and 49% to 75GWh, respectively, the analysts say. Its EV battery shipments should increase by 35% to 68Gwh in 2026, driven by a ramp-up of large-sized cylindrical batteries. Nomura lifts its 2025-2027 revenue forecasts for the battery manufacturer by 4%-11% on higher EV and ESS battery shipment assumptions. It raises the stock's target price to CNY62.00 from CNY58.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares last 2.8% higher at CNY49.87. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: India's Star Cement rises on unit getting mining lease
data.description:
** Star Cement rises as much as 3.6% to 286.56 rupees

** Stock set to gain for second straight month; up 15.7% so far in August

** Co's unit declared as preferred bidder for mining lease of Parewar limestone block in e-auctions conducted by Government of Rajasthan

** Co says block has area of 960 hectares with estimated limestone resource of 271.38 mln tonnes

** Stock rated "buy" on avg; median PT is 287.5 rupees, per data compiled by LSEG

data.title: U.S. Futures, European Stocks Fall
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were down 0.1% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined 0.1%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the UK market was closed. Other stocks in Europe were down. France's CAC 40 decreased 0.5% and Germany's DAX dropped 0.5%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index rose 0.1% to 95.16.

The German 10-year Bund yield climbed by 3 basis points to 2.759% from 2.727%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury rose by 1 basis point to 4.271% from 4.264%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were up as Japan's Nikkei 225 index gained 0.4%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng climbed 2%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 1.5%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Turkey's Sasa rises after establishing SASA RAFINERI for refinery and petrochemical investment
data.description:
** Turkish polyester maker Sasa shares jump around 7% after it decides to establish new company for refinery and petrochemical investment

** Sasa, a producer of polyester fibers, filament yarns and polymers, says it resolves to establish SASA RAFINERI, with a capital of 100 million lira ($2.44 million), to carry out its planned refinery and petrochemical facility investment in Adana/Yumurtalık

** The new company will also manage logistics, infrastructure, and ancillary production facilities, including the area designated as a Private Industrial Zone in Adana/Yumurtalık

** Brokerage Global Menkul Değerler say "We expect the impact of the development to be positive in the short term"

** Analyst Mustafa Kemal Eski from brokerage Marbaş Menkul Değerler said SASA's stock rose after the establishment of its refinery

** Eski noted the project is a key part of the region's operations, complementing Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET) and other investments, and expects it to bring significant benefits

($1 = 40.9755 liras)

data.title: SNF to acquire oilfield chemicals firm
data.description:
SNF Group said it has acquired Obsidian Chemical Solutions (Midland, Texas), a maker of completion chemicals for the oil and gas industry, primarily in the Permian Basin of Texas, as per Chemweek.

Terms of the transaction, including purchase price, were not disclosed.Obsidian “has one of the largest bulk storage capacities in the basin and a substantial manufacturing footprint,” SNF said. The acquisition will broaden SNF’s product portfolio with “customized chemical formulations for stimulation, acidizing, drill-out, cementing, and produced water treatment, enhancing asset integrity and optimizing production,” it added.The integration timeline for Obsidian “will be announced in due course,” according to SNF.

mrchub.com

data.title: Valneva shares tumble after suspension of U.S. chikungunya vaccine licence
data.description:
Shares in Valneva slumped around 25% on Monday after the French drugmaker said the United States Food and Drug Administration had suspended the licence for its chikungunya vaccine Ixchiq with immediate effect.

Valneva said the suspension follows four reports of serious adverse events consistent with the disease, and that it will have to immediately stop shipping and selling the vaccine in the U.S.

The company said it was evaluating the potential impact of permanently withdrawing the vaccine from the U.S. but would not change its financial outlook.

It said sales of Ixchiq accounted for 7.5 million euros ($8.78 million) in the first half of this year, or more than 8% of its total product sales.

Despite seeing the suspension as a setback, brokerage Kempen Daily said in a note that it views Ixchiq as only a minor reason to invest in Valneva.

"We see a negative share price reaction today as an attractive entry point," the brokerage added.

Chikungunya can cause high fever, joint pain and long-term disability and is spread primarily by the Aedes mosquito species, including the "tiger mosquito" which also transmits dengue.

Ixchiq, the first preventive vaccine for chikungunya, which was also approved in Europe, uses a weakened form of the virus to stimulate an immune response.

($1 = 0.8538 euros)

data.title: JDE Peet's stock soars on $18 billion KDP takeover
data.description:
** Shares in JDE Peet's jump as much as 17.8% on the agreement that Keurig Dr Pepper will acquire JDE Peets in an all cash offer of $18 billion

** The offer price implies a 20.2% premium to yesterday's closing price, KBC Securities says

** "We believe the acquisition of JDE Peet's by KDP makes sense," KBC notes and expects the closing to occur in the first half of 2026

** ING says the deal is expected to generate $400 million cost synergies and will create the "largest pure-play coffee company" with sales of $16 billion

** Neither broker sees regulatory approval being an issue seeing the fragmented coffee market and JDE Peets' limited U.S. market share

** At 07:43 GMT, shares in JDE Peets are trading up 17.4% to its highest price in almost three years

data.title: DAX Opens Week Lower
data.description:
The DAX in Frankfurt fell 0.5% to around 24,240 on Monday, trimming gains from the previous session as investors assessed recent comments from ECB President Christine Lagarde.

On Saturday, Lagarde noted that Europe’s labor market has exceeded expectations, remaining resilient despite high inflation and several sharp interest rate hikes in recent years.

Meanwhile, US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell signaled the possibility of near-term interest rate cuts last week.

Market participants have also been digesting new details on the US-EU trade agreement, which provided clarity for key sectors, including pharmaceuticals.

On the data front, traders will watch the Ifo Business Climate survey later today, along with retail sales, jobs data, and flash inflation readings on Friday.

On the bourse, top laggards included Siemens Energy (-1.8%), RWE (-1.6%), and E.ON (-0.9%).

  • FITCH ON INDIA - US TARIFFS ARE A MODERATE DOWNSIDE RISK TO OUR FORECAST, BUT ARE SUBJECT TO A HIGH DEGREE OF UNCERTAINTY.

data.title: China’s Sanfangxiang launches new PTA line
data.description:
Sanfangxiang (Jiangyin, China) has brought its new 1.5 million metric tons per year (MMt/y) purified terephthalic acid (PTA) line No. 2 online at Jiangsu, China, over Aug. 23-24, said Chemweek.

The source added that with both lines now in operation, the producer is running at around 95% of combined capacity.The Jiangsu-based facility comprises two PTA lines, each with a nameplate capacity of 1.5 MMt/y.Line 1 was reported to be operating at full rates as of July 30, Platts reported earlier.

mrchub.com

data.title: Kingboard Laminates 1H Rev HK$9.59B Vs. HK$8.64B >1888.HK
Kingboard Laminates 1H Net HK$933.3M Vs. Net HK$727.8M >1888.HK
Kingboard Laminates Declares Dividend of HK$0.15 >1888.HK
Euro zone yields rise, walking back Fed-driven fall
data.description:
Euro zone bond yields rose on Monday, reversing their fall from late last week as traders reassessed that U.S. Federal Reserve-driven move and its impact on Europe.

Germany's 10-year bond yield, the benchmark for the euro zone, was up nearly 4 basis points at 2.76%, (DE10YT=RR) having dropped a similar amount on Friday.

In his final address as Fed chair at the Jackson Hole economic symposium, Fed chair Jerome Powell hinted at a September interest rate cut. While he stopped short of committing, that was sufficient to spark a rally in stocks and U.S. Treasuries that spilled over into Europe.

That then reversed somewhat on Monday, partly because the European Central Bank, which has cut rates by much more than the Fed so far this cycle, is now in a different place.

Remarks by ECB policymakers at Jackson Hole were "consistent with an extended pause," Jim Reid, global head of macro research at Deutsche Bank, said in a note.

ECB President Christine Lagarde avoided discussing the policy outlook but highlighted the resilience of the euro area labour market.

Italy's 10-year yield moved largely in line with Germany's, up just over 4 bps to 3.60%.

Germany's two-year yield was up 2 bps at 1.97% (DE2YT=RR)
